analysis.consistency: The feature description is internally consistent, clearly outlining the mechanics for spell preparation and casting for the Druid class. The spell slot table aligns with standard D&D 5th Edition progression for a primary spellcasting class.
analysis.quality: The quality is high. It uses a clear table structure for the spell slots and provides a helpful, concrete example to illustrate the preparation mechanic (Wisdom modifier + Druid Level). The reference to the long rest for regaining slots and preparing new spells is standard and correct.

mechanics.spell_slot_progression: Standard full-caster progression, reaching 9th-level slots at level 17 (though the table correctly shows slots up to 20th level).
mechanics.spell_preparation_formula: Wisdom modifier + Druid Level (minimum of one spell). This is the correct preparation formula for Druids.
mechanics.casting_rules: Expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. Regain all expended slots after a long rest. These are standard casting rules.
mechanics.preparation_time: At least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on the list, which is the correct time investment for preparing spells during a long rest.

suggestions.detail: The description correctly implies that prepared spells can be cast using any higher-level slot, but explicitly confirming that spell slots are *expended* upon casting (which is done) is robust.
